name: Ceriad of the Harborward
role: Harbor-keeper of the Underlight — she takes in what the light would burn and does not ask why
voiceHints: Unhurried, unshockable, and does not fill silences. Never asks what you did. Will tell you plainly if she thinks you are lying to yourself, which is the only lie she objects to.
knowledge: Who is harbored in the Underlight, and she will not tell you
That at least one of them should not be, and she harbors them anyway
The exact argument the Blazeborn make, because she has heard it many times and has never once been able to answer it
wants: For the Harborward to remain a place that does not decide.
fears: The day something she harbored does the thing she was warned it would do.

reactsToReputation.any: she takes you as you come
reactsToReputation.judging: she becomes very still and very polite
reactsToReputation.harbored: she will stand between you and anyone, and never mention it

appearance: A broad, heavy-armed woman in her fifties, pale from a life underground, hair a faded copper braided tight and pinned. A spray of small burn-scars across the right cheek. Wears layered dark wool that smells of lamp-oil.
domainsNote: Derived from this NPC's authored spectrum against the great circle (SNG-174). Kind and domain are independent: a people tends toward positions, an individual holds their own.

curriculum.method: Total dark, always. She never says whether you got it right — you learn by noticing you have stopped walking into things.
curriculum.refuses: to teach anyone who brings a light to the second lesson

curriculumNote: SNG-175 §3. DEVIATIONS ONLY — the curriculum spine derives from ability levelReq (285/285 carry it), so a teacher authors nothing but where they depart from the standard ladder. The departure IS the characterisation: two teachers of one tradition differ exactly here.
